subject: Project Management
title: Post-Agile Thinking
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Post-Agile Thinking.

hook.text: Some thought leaders say we've moved beyond Agile — into something they call 'post-Agile.'

buildup.text: Post-Agile doesn't reject Agile — it absorbs it. Agility becomes a default, not a label.

discovery.text: Teams mix Scrum, Kanban, XP, and lean thinking without loyalty to any single framework.

twist.text: The manifesto's own authors say this was always the intent — principles over prescriptions.

climax.text: Post-Agile is what happens when Agile succeeds: it stops being special and just becomes normal.

quiz.question: What does 'post-Agile' mean?
options: Agility becomes a default mindset, not a labeled framework
